title: 3. DAEN > 7.1.8 Conclusions of the Analysis of Manufacturer Data (Continued)
text_content: Mitigations through product design and labeling are in place. The mitigations were derived from thorough risk analysis processes through which risk control measures are formulated, implemented, and validated during product development. The risk control measures have been tested through verification and validation activities, and through product usability studies. The risks and their associated potential hazards have been assessed via analysis of available clinical data regarding the use of HAL. The safety findings in the clinical data were analyzed in context of the benefits to the patients as described above. Clinical data was analyzed for any evidence of the residual risks of HAL 4.0 identified in the RMR report. No adverse events or clinical toxicities were reported as attributable to the residual risks outlined in Section 5.1.4 (e.g. radiation over/under exposure, collision).

title: 3.2.1 Scope, Schedule and Budget Control
text_content: Scope control will consist of managing requirement changes, enhancements, feature requests and scope changes.  Each will be controlled as outlined below. Requirement changes: If all of these conditions are met:  The changes do not impact the essential functionality and/or user experience of the device  The changed do not impact the program schedule committed to MER by more than two calendar weeks Then the changes will be managed through the document control process using Review Report Forms (LL40). If the above conditions are not all met, then the requirement change is elevated to a change in scope by definition.  See Scope Change section below. Enhancements and Feature Requests: Any requirement changes to implement additional features or enhancements, after Elaboration Phase, will be collected as TFS backlog items reporting to an Enhancement EPIC. source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 7.0 Verification Activities > 7.1 Risk control measures
text_content: The three risk control options shall be considered in their order of priority: o Inherently safe design and manufacture; o Protective measures in the device itself or in the manufacturing process; o Information for safety (warnings/precautions/contra-indications) and, where appropriate, training to users. All recognized risk control measures shall be implemented to reduce risk to or maintain risk at an acceptable level. Implementation of risk control measures shall continue until successive risk control options or measures do not further reduce the estimated risk. Whenever possible, requirements of international standards or their corresponding harmonized versions shall be utilized as risk control measures. These applied standards are listed or referenced in Appendix B of this RMP. The implementation of risk control measures shall be verified. The effectiveness of the implemented risk control measures shall be verified or, as appropriate, validated.

title: 3. DAEN > 7.1.4 Risk Management Analyses (Continued)
text_content: device defects that could result in a significantly inaccurate treatment are likely to be identified and remedied by the physician and the treatment team before the device is used to treat the patient. This characteristic contributes significantly to the overall safety of the devices in a clinical context. Potential hazards identified in the RMR were analyzed for effective mitigation measures, which were then implemented via system design during product development, including product labeling. The RMR and its appendix/appendices and associated documentation describe the hazards, use cases, potential harms, pre- and post- mitigation hazard frequency, severity, and overall risk level, and Risk Control Measures (RCMs). The table(s) also provide(s) system requirement specifications related to the risks or hazards, and test case IDs for verification and validations testing. Safety features have been validated as a part of the Halcyon Verification and Validation processes

source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 8.0 Production and Post-production Information
text_content: Production and post-production information will be continually monitored and analyzed to determine if new risks have been identified and to review the assessments of known risks to maintain an effective risk management process. This information includes, but is not limited to, design and process changes, revisions of standards, identifications of defects, and reports of complaints. In addition to Varian’s information about the device, the following shall be reviewed: • Information on similar devices on the market; • Publicly available information such as recalls, FDA Medical Device Reports, and vigilance reports; • Scientific literature. Analysis of relevant data is used to demonstrate that risk-based decisions and risk control measures for this device are appropriate and effective. title: 1.0 Introduction > 1.2     Scope
text_content: This document evaluates the applicability of the product-specific standards, laws, and/or directives that must be met to obtain and maintain market access in target markets.  Additional standards may be included for evaluation based upon customer requests and commercial considerations. This document identifies the product-specific standards, laws, and/or directives that apply to the entire device or product. Project-specific requirements and timing may be specified in the Regulatory Plan and the Design and Development Plan. Regulations applicable to Varian Products 1.3 The product-specific standards, laws, and/or directives; and guidance documents listed within this record are used to demonstrate product compliance and/or conformity. The regulations with which Varian establishes its quality system and for which our company is audited and certified (ISO 13485, MDSAP) for ongoing compliance are maintained in the Varian Master Regulations and Standards List.

title: 2. “Ethos Radiotherapy System ” > 9.5 China Marketing Clearance Strategy > 9.5.1 China documentation requirements (Continued)
text_content: A catalogue for the submitted application dossiers shall be provided, including all titles and subtitles of this chapter, catalogue serial number, catalogue title, applicable conditions, uploaded file name, and uploaded file page number, as well as the page number of all the contents in the catalogue. As to the applicability, whether the CR catalogue is applicable shall be indicated. DTR+200days China RA CH3.2 Risk Management R Risk Management Data of Products Product risk management information related to product changes. Risk management data of products is the data formed by recording the product risk management process and its review results. The following contents shall be provided and the traceability of the following processes for each hazard identified shall be explained. 1. Risk analysis: including identification of scope of application of medical device and safety-related characteristics, identification of hazards and estimation of risk of each hazardous situation.
